steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure around the blank
steps.content: After that comes a prepositional phrase (through community involvement and scientific study) immediately before the main verb of the relative clause (aim).

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
Identify the structure: "conservation efforts [BLANK] through community involvement and scientific study, aim to bring adjutants back..." — the blank follows "efforts" and introduces a relative clause ("that... aim to bring"). The prepositional phrase "through community involvement and scientific study" is parenthetical within the relative clause, already closed by the comma before "aim." The blank needs a comma after "that" to open that parenthetical bracket.
  • Choice A — that—: "efforts that— through community involvement and scientific study, aim to bring..." — an em dash opens a parenthetical, but the closing punctuation is a comma, creating a mismatch. ✗
  • Choice B — that;: "efforts that; through community involvement and scientific study, aim to bring..." — semicolon after "that" is never grammatical; it would split the relative clause from its antecedent. ✗
  • Choice C — that:: "efforts that: through community involvement and scientific study, aim to bring..." — colon after a relative pronoun is not a valid structure. ✗
  • Choice D — that,: "efforts that, through community involvement and scientific study, aim to bring..." — comma opens the parenthetical phrase, matching the closing comma before "aim"; the relative clause reads correctly. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
